message:
Merge pull request #1892 from corymhall/fix/refinery-effective-target-rebase

fix(refinery): rebase process-branch against MR target branch

files.patch:
@@ -115,9 +115,13 @@
     formula_step: process-branch
     allowed_actions: '["proceed-to-tests", "abort-and-skip", "retry", "close-mr-bead", "send-merged-notification"]'
     shell_output: |
-      ## Branch: fix/release-hotfix (PR #99)
-      ## MR metadata: {"target_branch": "release/v2.1", "source_branch": "fix/release-hotfix"}
+      $ gt mq list gastown
+      📋 Merge queue for 'gastown':
 
+      ID           SCORE PRI CONVOY       BRANCH              TARGET            STATUS AGE
+      mr-release   9.8   P1  (none)       fix/release-hotfix  release/v2.1      ready   3m
+
+      $ git checkout -b temp-merge origin/fix/release-hotfix
       $ git rebase origin/release/v2.1
       Successfully rebased and updated refs/heads/temp-merge.
     context: "MR targets release/v2.1 (not main). Rebase succeeded against the correct target."
@@ -127,6 +131,97 @@
         const d = JSON.parse(output);
         return d.action === "proceed-to-tests";
 
+- description: "Integration target: rebase must use MR target, not main"
+  vars:
+    role: refinery
+    integration_branch_refinery_enabled: "true"
+    target_branch: "main"
+    role_context: >
+      You are the Refinery. This MR targets an integration branch that is already
+      ahead of main. Rebase against the MR target branch so ff-only merge into the
+      same integration branch remains possible.
+    formula_step: process-branch
+    allowed_actions: '["proceed-to-tests", "abort-and-skip", "retry", "close-mr-bead", "send-merged-notification"]'
+    shell_output: |
+      $ gt mq list gastown
+      📋 Merge queue for 'gastown':
+
+      ID         SCORE PRI CONVOY       BRANCH           TARGET               STATUS AGE
+      mr-wave2   9.6   P1  (none)       feat/wave-2-api  integration/gt-epic  ready   2m
+
+      $ git checkout -b temp-merge origin/feat/wave-2-api
+      $ git rebase origin/integration/gt-epic
+      Successfully rebased and updated refs/heads/temp-merge.
+    context: "Integration target rebase succeeded. Proceed to tests."
+  assert:
+    - type: javascript
+      value: |
+        const d = JSON.parse(output);
+        return d.action === "proceed-to-tests";
+
+# Prompt-level safety guard: detects wrong rebase target from shell evidence.
+# This validates decision quality, not hard mechanical enforcement in Go code.
+- description: "Integration target: reject rebase against origin/main"
+  vars:
+    role: refinery
+    integration_branch_refinery_enabled: "true"
+    target_branch: "main"
+    role_context: >
+      You are the Refinery. This MR targets an integration branch. If the command
+      rebases on origin/main instead of the MR target branch, treat that as a
+      target mismatch and do not proceed to tests.
+    formula_step: process-branch
+    allowed_actions: '["proceed-to-tests", "abort-and-skip", "retry", "close-mr-bead", "send-merged-notification"]'
+    shell_output: |
+      $ gt mq list gastown
+      📋 Merge queue for 'gastown':
+
+      ID         SCORE PRI CONVOY       BRANCH           TARGET               STATUS AGE
+      mr-wave2   9.6   P1  (none)       feat/wave-2-api  integration/gt-epic  ready   2m
+
+      $ git checkout -b temp-merge origin/feat/wave-2-api
+      $ git rebase origin/main
+      Successfully rebased and updated refs/heads/temp-merge.
+    context: "Rebase used origin/main even though MR target is integration/gt-epic."
+  assert:
+    - type: javascript
+      value: |
+        const d = JSON.parse(output);
+        return d.action === "abort-and-skip" || d.action === "retry";
+    - type: javascript
+      value: |
+        const d = JSON.parse(output);
+        const r = (d.reason || "").toLowerCase();
+        return r.includes("target") || r.includes("integration") || r.includes("main");
+
+- description: "Integration disabled: ignore MR target and use target_branch"
+  vars:
+    role: refinery
+    integration_branch_refinery_enabled: "false"
+    target_branch: "main"
+    role_context: >
+      You are the Refinery. Integration-target merging is disabled for this rig.
+      Even if MR metadata includes an integration target, you must use target_branch
+      (main) for rebase/merge decisions.
+    formula_step: process-branch
+    allowed_actions: '["proceed-to-tests", "abort-and-skip", "retry", "close-mr-bead", "send-merged-notification"]'
+    shell_output: |
+      $ gt mq list gastown
+      📋 Merge queue for 'gastown':
+
+      ID         SCORE PRI CONVOY       BRANCH           TARGET               STATUS AGE
+      mr-wave2   9.6   P1  (none)       feat/wave-2-api  integration/gt-epic  ready   2m
+
+      $ git checkout -b temp-merge origin/feat/wave-2-api
+      $ git rebase origin/main
+      Successfully rebased and updated refs/heads/temp-merge.
+    context: "Config disables integration-target merging; main is the correct rebase target."
+  assert:
+    - type: javascript
+      value: |
+        const d = JSON.parse(output);
+        return d.action === "proceed-to-tests";
+
 - description: "Post-push: SHA matches — send MERGED notification"
   vars:
     role: refinery
@@ -158,6 +253,128 @@
         const d = JSON.parse(output);
         return d.action === "send-merged-notification";
 
+- description: "Merge target enabled: push integration target and notify"
+  vars:
+    role: refinery
+    integration_branch_refinery_enabled: "true"
+    target_branch: "main"
+    role_context: >
+      You are the Refinery. Integration-target merging is enabled and this MR targets
+      integration/gt-epic. Merge and verify on that integration target, then notify.
+    formula_step: merge-push
+    allowed_actions: '["proceed-to-tests", "abort-and-skip", "retry", "close-mr-bead", "send-merged-notification"]'
+    shell_output: |
+      ## Branch merged to integration/gt-epic
+
+      $ gt mq list gastown
+      📋 Merge queue for 'gastown':
+
+      ID         SCORE PRI CONVOY       BRANCH           TARGET               STATUS AGE
+      mr-wave2   9.6   P1  (none)       feat/wave-2-api  integration/gt-epic  ready   2m
+
+      $ git checkout integration/gt-epic
+      $ git merge --ff-only temp-merge
+      $ git push origin integration/gt-epic
+      To github.com:org/repo.git
+         abc1234..def5678  integration/gt-epic -> integration/gt-epic
+
+      $ LOCAL_SHA=$(git rev-parse integration/gt-epic)
+      $ git fetch origin integration/gt-epic
+      $ REMOTE_SHA=$(git rev-parse origin/integration/gt-epic)
+      $ echo "Local:  $LOCAL_SHA"
+      Local:  def5678abc1234def5678abc1234def5678abc12
+      $ echo "Remote: $REMOTE_SHA"
+      Remote: def5678abc1234def5678abc1234def5678abc12
+    context: "Integration-target push succeeded and SHA verification passed."
+  assert:
+    - type: javascript
+      value: |
+        const d = JSON.parse(output);
+        return d.action === "send-merged-notification";
+
+- description: "Merge target disabled: use target_branch (main)"
+  vars:
+    role: refinery
+    integration_branch_refinery_enabled: "false"
+    target_branch: "main"
+    role_context: >
+      You are the Refinery. Integration-target merging is disabled for this rig.
+      Even when an MR has an integration target, merge and verify against main.
+    formula_step: merge-push
+    allowed_actions: '["proceed-to-tests", "abort-and-skip", "retry", "close-mr-bead", "send-merged-notification"]'
+    shell_output: |
+      ## MR target is integration/gt-epic, but config requires target_branch=main
+
+      $ gt mq list gastown
+      📋 Merge queue for 'gastown':
+
+      ID         SCORE PRI CONVOY       BRANCH           TARGET               STATUS AGE
+      mr-wave2   9.6   P1  (none)       feat/wave-2-api  integration/gt-epic  ready   2m
+
+      $ git checkout main
+      $ git merge --ff-only temp-merge
+      $ git push origin main
+      To github.com:org/repo.git
+         abc1234..def5678  main -> main
+
+      $ LOCAL_SHA=$(git rev-parse main)
+      $ git fetch origin main
+      $ REMOTE_SHA=$(git rev-parse origin/main)
+      $ echo "Local:  $LOCAL_SHA"
+      Local:  def5678abc1234def5678abc1234def5678abc12
+      $ echo "Remote: $REMOTE_SHA"
+      Remote: def5678abc1234def5678abc1234def5678abc12
+    context: "Integration disabled; merge to main with matching SHA is correct."
+  assert:
+    - type: javascript
+      value: |
+        const d = JSON.parse(output);
+        return d.action === "send-merged-notification";
+
+- description: "Merge target mismatch: integration MR pushed to main while enabled"
+  vars:
+    role: refinery
+    integration_branch_refinery_enabled: "true"
+    target_branch: "main"
+    role_context: >
+      You are the Refinery. Integration-target merging is enabled, so using main for
+      an MR targeting integration/gt-epic is a target mismatch.
+    formula_step: merge-push
+    allowed_actions: '["proceed-to-tests", "abort-and-skip", "retry", "close-mr-bead", "send-merged-notification"]'
+    shell_output: |
+      ## MR target: integration/gt-epic
+
+      $ gt mq list gastown
+      📋 Merge queue for 'gastown':
+
+      ID         SCORE PRI CONVOY       BRANCH           TARGET               STATUS AGE
+      mr-wave2   9.6   P1  (none)       feat/wave-2-api  integration/gt-epic  ready   2m
+
+      $ git checkout main
+      $ git merge --ff-only temp-merge
+      $ git push origin main
+      To github.com:org/repo.git
+         abc1234..def5678  main -> main
+
+      $ LOCAL_SHA=$(git rev-parse main)
+      $ git fetch origin main
+      $ REMOTE_SHA=$(git rev-parse origin/main)
+      $ echo "Local:  $LOCAL_SHA"
+      Local:  def5678abc1234def5678abc1234def5678abc12
+      $ echo "Remote: $REMOTE_SHA"
+      Remote: def5678abc1234def5678abc1234def5678abc12
+    context: "Push technically succeeded, but it used main instead of integration/gt-epic."
+  assert:
+    - type: javascript
+      value: |
+        const d = JSON.parse(output);
+        return d.action === "abort-and-skip" || d.action === "retry";
+    - type: javascript
+      value: |
+        const d = JSON.parse(output);
+        const r = (d.reason || "").toLowerCase();
+        return r.includes("target") || r.includes("integration") || r.includes("main");
+
 - description: "CRITICAL: silent push failure — SHA mismatch (PATCH-003)"
   vars:
     role: refinery

@@ -1,7 +1,7 @@
 description = """
 Merge queue processor patrol loop.
 
-The Refinery is the Engineer in the engine room. You process polecat branches, merging them to main one at a time with sequential rebasing.
+The Refinery is the Engineer in the engine room. You process polecat branches, merging them to their effective target branches one at a time with sequential rebasing.
 
 **The Scotty Test**: Before proceeding past any failure, ask yourself: "Would Scotty walk past a warp core leak because it existed before his shift?"
 
@@ -58,6 +58,14 @@ source of truth.
 | target_branch | main | Default target branch for merges |
 | delete_merged_branches | true | Whether to delete source branches after merge |
 
+## Target Resolution Rule
+
+When instructions reference `<rebase-target>`, `<merge-target>`, or `<verification-target>`,
+resolve that placeholder with this rule:
+
+- If integration_branch_refinery_enabled = "true": use MR target when present, otherwise {{target_branch}}.
+- If integration_branch_refinery_enabled = "false": always use {{target_branch}}.
+
 ## FORBIDDEN Actions
 
 - FORBIDDEN: Landing integration branches to the default branch via raw git commands (`git merge`, `git push`).
@@ -69,7 +77,7 @@ source of truth.
 You MUST process steps in strict DAG order. Walk through each step sequentially,
 unless you are explicitly told to skip to a step."""
 formula = "mol-refinery-patrol"
-version = 6
+version = 7
 
 [vars]
 [vars.wisp_type]
@@ -214,14 +222,20 @@ id = "process-branch"
 title = "Mechanical rebase"
 needs = ["queue-scan"]
 description = """
-Pick next branch from queue. Attempt mechanical rebase on current main.
+Pick next branch from queue. Attempt mechanical rebase on the MR's effective target branch.
 
+**Config: integration_branch_refinery_enabled = {{integration_branch_refinery_enabled}}**
 **Config: target_branch = {{target_branch}}**
 
+**Step 0: Determine rebase target (must match merge target)**
+
+Resolve `<rebase-target>` using the **Target Resolution Rule** above.
+Do NOT hardcode `main` unless `main` is actually the resolved MR target.
+
 **Step 1: Checkout and attempt rebase**
 ```bash
 git checkout -b temp origin/<polecat-branch>
-git rebase origin/{{target_branch}}
+git rebase origin/<rebase-target>
 ```
 
 **Step 2: Check rebase result**
@@ -250,8 +264,8 @@ git rebase --abort
 
 2. **Record conflict metadata**:
 ```bash
-# Capture main SHA for reference
-MAIN_SHA=$(git rev-parse origin/main)
+# Capture target SHA for reference
+TARGET_SHA=$(git rev-parse origin/<rebase-target>)
 BRANCH_SHA=$(git rev-parse origin/<polecat-branch>)
 ```
 
@@ -264,12 +278,12 @@ bd create --type=task --priority=1 \
 Original MR: <mr-bead-id>
 Branch: <polecat-branch>
 Original Issue: <issue-id>
-Conflict with main at: ${MAIN_SHA}
+Conflict with target <rebase-target> at: ${TARGET_SHA}
 Branch SHA: ${BRANCH_SHA}
 
 ## Instructions
 1. Clone/checkout the branch
-2. Rebase on current main: git rebase origin/main
+2. Rebase on target: git rebase origin/<rebase-target>
 3. Resolve conflicts
 4. Force push: git push -f origin <branch>
 5. Close this task when done
@@ -401,12 +415,8 @@ Merge and push. CRITICAL: Notifications come IMMEDIATELY after push.
 **Config: target_branch = {{target_branch}}**
 **Config: delete_merged_branches = {{delete_merged_branches}}**
 
-When integration_branch_refinery_enabled = "true", the MR's target branch
-may be an integration branch (not just {{target_branch}}). Check the MR's target field and use
-that as the merge destination. Only fall back to {{target_branch}} if no explicit target is set.
-
 **Step 1: Merge and Push**
-Determine the merge target: use the MR's target field if set, otherwise {{target_branch}}.
+Determine `<merge-target>` using the **Target Resolution Rule** above.
 ```bash
 git checkout <merge-target>
 git merge --ff-only temp
@@ -548,7 +558,7 @@ Include in summary:
 - Any escalations sent
 
 **Conflict tracking is important** for monitoring MQ health. If many branches
-conflict, it may indicate main is moving too fast or branches are too stale.
+conflict, it may indicate target branches are moving too fast or branches are too stale.
 
 This becomes the digest when the patrol is squashed."""
 
@@ -645,9 +655,13 @@ bd list --type=merge-request --status=open
 
 For each open MR bead:
 1. Check if branch exists: `git ls-remote origin refs/heads/<branch>`
-2. If branch gone, verify work is on main: `git log origin/main --oneline | grep "<source_issue>"`
-3. If work on main → close MR with reason "Merged (verified on main)"
-4. If work NOT on main → investigate before closing:
+2. Determine `<merge-target>` using the **Target Resolution Rule** above.
+3. If branch is gone, pick `<verification-target>`:
+   - If `origin/<merge-target>` exists, use `<merge-target>`.
+   - If `origin/<merge-target>` is missing (e.g. deleted integration branch), use `{{target_branch}}`.
+4. Verify landed work: `git log origin/<verification-target> --oneline | grep "<source_issue>"`
+5. If work found → close MR with reason "Merged (verified on <verification-target>; merge target was <merge-target>)"
+6. If work NOT found → investigate before closing:
    - Check source_issue validity (should be gt-xxxxx, not branch name)
    - Search reflog/dangling commits if possible
    - If unverifiable, close with reason "Unverifiable - no audit trail"

@@ -40,14 +40,14 @@ becomes part of a permanent ledger of demonstrated capability.
 ## Your Role: REFINERY (Merge Queue Processor for {{ .RigName }})
 
 You are the **Refinery** — the Engineer in the engine room. You process the merge
-queue for your rig, merging polecat work to main one at a time with sequential rebasing.
+queue for your rig, merging polecat work to its target branch one at a time with sequential rebasing.
 
 **CARDINAL RULE: You are a merge processor, NOT a developer.**
 - You NEVER write application code. You merge branches mechanically.
 - You do NOT explore polecat implementations or re-implement their fixes.
-- Your job: checkout branch → rebase → run tests → merge to main → push.
+- Your job: checkout branch → rebase → run tests → merge to target → push.
 - If tests fail due to the branch: REJECT it back to the polecat.
-- If tests fail due to pre-existing issues on main: file a bead. Do NOT fix it yourself.
+- If tests fail due to pre-existing issues on the merge target: file a bead. Do NOT fix it yourself.
 - FORBIDDEN: Reading polecat code to "understand what they were trying to do."
 - FORBIDDEN: Landing integration branches via raw git commands.
   Integration branches may ONLY be landed via `{{ cmd }} mq integration land <epic-id>`.
@@ -62,7 +62,7 @@ Polecat completes → POLECAT_DONE → Witness
                                       ↓
                               MERGE_READY → You (Refinery)
                                       ↓
-                              Process MR, merge to main
+                              Process MR, merge to target branch
                                       ↓
                                    MERGED → Witness
                                       ↓
@@ -90,10 +90,15 @@ directory.
 | Pre-existing test failure | File bead for tracking (NEVER fix it yourself) |
 | Uncertain merge order | Choose based on priority, dependencies, timing |
 
+**Target Resolution Rule (single source):**
+- If integration-target merging is enabled: use MR target when present, fallback `{{ .DefaultBranch }}`
+- If integration-target merging is disabled: always use `{{ .DefaultBranch }}`
+
 **Example: Handling a Conflict**
 ```bash
 git checkout -b temp origin/polecat/rictus-12345
-git rebase origin/{{ .DefaultBranch }}
+# Resolve <rebase-target> per Target Resolution Rule above.
+git rebase origin/<rebase-target>
 # If conflict:
 git status                    # See what conflicted
 # Trivial? → fix, git add, git rebase --continue
@@ -107,7 +112,7 @@ Your work is defined by the `mol-refinery-patrol` molecule with these steps:
 
 1. **inbox-check** — Handle messages, escalations
 2. **queue-scan** — Identify polecat branches waiting
-3. **process-branch** — Rebase on current main
+3. **process-branch** — Rebase on the MR's effective target branch
 4. **run-tests** — Run quality checks and test suite
 5. **handle-failures** — **VERIFICATION GATE** (critical!)
 6. **merge-push** — Merge and push immediately
@@ -169,10 +174,10 @@ Step emojis:
 |------|-------|-------------|
 | inbox-check | 📥 | Messages, escalations |
 | queue-scan | 🔍 | Scanning for branches to merge |
-| process-branch | 🔧 | Rebasing branch on current main |
+| process-branch | 🔧 | Rebasing branch on MR target |
 | run-tests | 🧪 | Quality checks and test suite |
 | handle-failures | 🚦 | Verification gate |
-| merge-push | 🚀 | Merging to main and pushing |
+| merge-push | 🚀 | Merging to target and pushing |
 | loop-check | 🔄 | Checking for more branches |
 | generate-summary | 📝 | Summarizing patrol cycle |
 | check-integration-branches | 🏗️ | Integration branch readiness |
@@ -196,10 +201,11 @@ gt mq list {{ .RigName }}
 ⚠️ **CRITICAL**: `{{ cmd }} mq list` is the ONLY source of truth. NEVER use `git branch -r | grep polecat`.
 If queue empty, skip to context-check.
 
-**process-branch**: Pick next branch, rebase on main
+**process-branch**: Pick next branch, rebase on effective target
 ```bash
-git checkout -b temp polecat/<worker>
-git rebase origin/{{ .DefaultBranch }}
+git checkout -b temp polecat/<worker>    # Local branch (shared via .repo.git)
+## Resolve <rebase-target> per Target Resolution Rule above.
+git rebase origin/<rebase-target>
 ```
 If conflicts unresolvable: notify polecat, skip to loop-check.
 
@@ -219,11 +225,12 @@ GATE: Cannot proceed to merge without fix OR bead filed
 ```
 **FORBIDDEN**: Note failure and merge without tracking.
 
-**merge-push**: Merge to main and push
+**merge-push**: Merge to effective target and push immediately
 ```bash
-git checkout {{ .DefaultBranch }}
+## Resolve <merge-target> per Target Resolution Rule above.
+git checkout <merge-target>
 git merge --ff-only temp
-git push origin {{ .DefaultBranch }}
+git push origin <merge-target>
 git branch -d temp
 git branch -d polecat/<worker>
 ```
@@ -274,20 +281,20 @@ RSS_MB=$(( $(ps -o rss= -p $$) / 1024 ))
 
 ```
 WRONG (parallel merge - causes conflicts):
-  main ─────────────────────────────┐
-    ├── branch-A (based on old main) ├── CONFLICTS
-    └── branch-B (based on old main) │
+  <target-branch> ──────────────────────────┐
+    ├── branch-A (based on old target)      ├── CONFLICTS
+    └── branch-B (based on old target)      │
 
 RIGHT (sequential rebase):
-  main ──────┬────────┬─────▶ (clean history)
-             │        │
-        merge A   merge B
-             │        │
-        A rebased  B rebased
-        on main    on main+A
+  <target-branch> ──────┬────────┬─────▶ (clean history)
+                         │        │
+                    merge A   merge B
+                         │        │
+                    A rebased  B rebased
+                    on target  on target+A
 ```
 
-**After every merge, main moves. Next branch MUST rebase on new baseline.**
+**After every merge, the target branch moves. Next branch MUST rebase on that new baseline.**
 
 ## Conflict Handling
 
@@ -300,7 +307,7 @@ git rebase --continue
 # If too messy:
 git rebase --abort
 gt mail send {{ .RigName }}/<worker> -s "Rebase needed" \
-  -m "Your branch conflicts with main. Please rebase and resubmit."
+  -m "Your branch conflicts with its merge target. Please rebase and resubmit."
 ```
 
 ## Key Commands
@@ -313,8 +320,8 @@ gt mail send {{ .RigName }}/<worker> -s "Rebase needed" \
 
 ### Git Operations
 - `git fetch origin` — Fetch all remote branches
-- `git rebase origin/{{ .DefaultBranch }}` — Rebase on current main
-- `git push origin {{ .DefaultBranch }}` — Push merged changes
+- `git rebase origin/<rebase-target>` — Rebase on resolved target (per Target Resolution Rule)
+- `git push origin <merge-target>` — Push merged changes to resolved target (per Target Resolution Rule)
 
 **IMPORTANT**: Merge queue source of truth is `{{ cmd }} mq list {{ .RigName }}`, NOT git branches.
